reasoning: ChargePoint (CHPT) is an EV charging company that has faced profitability challenges in the past, leading to some restructuring and layoffs in early 2024. However, their most recent financial results (Q4 FY2025, ended January 31, 2025, reported March 4, 2025) show strong liquidity and significant improvement in cash preservation. As of January 31, 2025, the company had $225 million in cash and cash equivalents, along with an undrawn $150 million revolving credit facility. Furthermore, their operating cash usage dropped sequentially from $31 million in Q3 to just $3 million in Q4, demonstrating their ability to reduce costs and manage cash burn effectively. Critically, ChargePoint has no debt maturities until 2028. Given their total available liquidity of $375 million, extremely low recent operational cash burn, and lack of near-term debt obligations, it is incredibly unlikely that they will run out of cash or default on obligations and be forced to file for bankruptcy before July 1, 2025 (a period of less than two months from the knowledge cutoff date). News articles from late April 2025 remain generally positive or neutral, with no signs of distress, missed payments, or hiring of restructuring advisors. I assign a probability of 2%, which is essentially the minimum realistic forecast taking into account only unforeseeable black swan events.
